blocks.normalized_text: When Embracer wrote down $2.1B last quarter, the surviving indie studios noticed a different number first: the MG. Recoupment terms that would have been 'competitive' in 2022 are now the ceiling, not the floor. The writedown itself isn't structural; the reclassification is.

For the studios that signed in 2024-2025, this isn't news. They lived through the conversion. But for anyone entering negotiations now, the deal shape has hardened in ways that weren't visible when Embracer announced.

The structural change has three components, and they compound. First, MG itself has been reclassified as a conditional liability under post-Embracer accounting. Second, recoupment terms have hardened.
Third — and this is the piece most studios miss — conditional advances default to lower thresholds when the publisher claims structural risk.
If you're signing in the next 18 months, assume the floor is now the ceiling. Negotiate against that, not against the 2022 numbers.
